title: plagiobothrys collinus var. ursinus
intro: Plagiobothrys collinus var. ursinus, also known as the "Brown Alumroot" or "Fawn-lily-of-the-valley," is a plant species within the Boraginaceae family, characterized by its small, white or purple flowers and hairy, fleshy roots. Traditionally, the plant's roots have been used in herbal medicine due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Plagiobothrys collinus var. ursinus are believed to provide health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing swelling, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and preparing the roots for use in teas, tinctures, or infusions.
